methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $312.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $312.5B

private.name: SandboxAQ
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $5.75B

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

data.narrative:
Crusoe Energy, a Series E AI & Software company, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) including Palantir, SandboxAQ, Ayar Labs, Waymo, and Figure AI. The target has raised $3B+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $10.0B.

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, applying a standard 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $10.0B last post-money valuation, implies $14.0B. Peer-median triangulation across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—SandboxAQ ($5.8B), Ayar Labs ($3.8B), and Palantir ($312.5B)—yields a median of $5.8B, though the range spans $3.8B to $312.5B, reflecting material dispersion in the peer set.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $4.9B (low) to $16.1B (high), with a median of $9.9B. The current $10.0B valuation sits within this range and approximates the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations—particularly Palantir's $312.5B outlier—and the limited applicability of stage-progression given the heterogeneity of Series E outcomes in frontier AI. The peer-median method carries elevated uncertainty due to small sample size and sectoral heterogeneity. Revenue multiples and path-to-profitability metrics would strengthen the analysis.
